h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Okay, so `city` is the field name, and `Chicago` is the answer in that field for this row.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to practice identifying headers and values in subsequent columns.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor maintains the same pedagogical strategy and task structure throughout the dialogue without any reversal or adaptation.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no learner pivot turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no learner pivot turn for the tutor to adapt to.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, so x-axis: “Time (weeks)” means variable = time, unit = weeks?

scored.statedInsightEvidence: The key strip under it helps: rabbit is the food, so rabbit to hawk, point on hawk.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to practice the procedure of drawing arrows from food to consumer using the key strip.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: [sets the key strip under the rabbit-hawk pair, then turns the pencil so the eraser is the tail and the point is the head]
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor adapts to the learner's struggle with the 'chasing' intuition by physically manipulating the pencil and key strip to represent the arrow's tail and head.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.
